defects.reasoning: Continuous sediment accumulation is clearly visible at the bottom of the pipe. Based on the ratio of the sediment height to the pipe diameter, it occupies approximately 30% of the cross-section, falling within the 20% to 40% range defined for Grade 3 Sedimentation (CJ).
defects.name: 沉积

observation.material_and_diameter: HDPE material, nominal diameter 200mm as indicated by the overlay text.
observation.lighting_and_water: Poor lighting and high turbidity/blurriness in the foreground. Water and sediment level is high at the bottom of the pipe.
observation.overall_profile: The pipe maintains its circular shape in the visible background portion, but the lower half is obscured by accumulation.
observation.pipe_bottom: Continuous and thick accumulation of silt/sediment along the bottom of the pipe.
observation.pipe_wall: The upper pipe wall appears intact with no clear signs of fracture or deformation, though visibility is limited by camera focus and distance.
observation.joints: No obvious joint displacement or material protrusion is visible in the clear section.
observation.abnormal_details: Silt and sandy deposits have accumulated continuously along the bottom of the pipe. The depth of the sediment is estimated to be between 20% and 40% of the pipe diameter (approximately 30%).
